Package com.salesforce.multicloudj.blob.driver

Class ObjectRetentionConfig

java.lang.Object
com.salesforce.multicloudj.blob.driver.ObjectRetentionConfig
public class ObjectRetentionConfig extends Object
Configuration for updating per-object retention via BlobStore.updateObjectRetention(String, String, ObjectRetentionConfig).

Distinct from ObjectLockConfiguration, which is used at upload time and additionally carries legal-hold flags. This type is scoped strictly to retention update: changing the retention mode and/or the retention expiration date on an object that already has retention configured. Legal hold remains on BlobStore.updateLegalHold(String, String, boolean).

Field semantics

  • mode — target retention mode. May be null to preserve the object's current retention mode (the provider fetches the current mode and reuses it).
  • retainUntilDate — new retention expiration date. Must be non-null. Sub-millisecond precision is not preserved on GCP (GCS truncates to RFC 3339 millisecond precision); for retention dates this is generally not a concern.
  • bypassGovernanceRetention — when true, allows shortening or removing retention on objects currently in RetentionMode.GOVERNANCE (AWS) or UNLOCKED (GCP) mode. Maps to bypassGovernanceRetention on AWS and to overrideUnlockedRetention on GCP. The flag is ignored on the immutable mode (RetentionMode.COMPLIANCE / LOCKED); both clouds reject any shorten or downgrade attempt server-side regardless of this flag.

The complete (mode, bypass, current state) → outcome rules table is documented on BlobStore.updateObjectRetention(String, String, ObjectRetentionConfig).

  • Method Details

    • builder

      public static ObjectRetentionConfig.ObjectRetentionConfigBuilder builder()

    • getMode

      public RetentionMode getMode()
      Target retention mode. May be null to preserve the current mode of the object.

      Mode downgrades from RetentionMode.COMPLIANCE to RetentionMode.GOVERNANCE are rejected by every provider — the underlying clouds (AWS S3, GCP GCS) do not permit such transitions, and MultiCloudJ surfaces a uniform FailedPreconditionException for the attempt.

    • getRetainUntilDate

      public Instant getRetainUntilDate()
      New retention expiration date. Must be non-null.

      Sub-millisecond precision is not preserved on GCP — GCS truncates this value to RFC 3339 millisecond precision on the wire.

    • getBypassGovernanceRetention

      public Boolean getBypassGovernanceRetention()
      When true, allows shortening or removing retention on objects currently in RetentionMode.GOVERNANCE (AWS) / UNLOCKED (GCP) mode.

      Maps to bypassGovernanceRetention on AWS PutObjectRetention requests and to Storage.BlobTargetOption.overrideUnlockedRetention(true) on GCP storage.update calls.

      No effect on the immutable mode. Both AWS and GCP reject any shorten, downgrade, or remove attempt against RetentionMode.COMPLIANCE / LOCKED regardless of this flag, IAM permissions, or principal (including AWS root). MultiCloudJ mirrors this server-side semantic with a client-side guard for uniform error reporting.

      null or false ⇒ no bypass (default).
